title: glyceria aquatica subsp. arundinacea
intro: Glyceria aquatica subsp. arundinacea, also known as tall mannagrass, is a perennial wetland grass native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its tall, slender stems and feathery leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Glyceria aquatica subsp. arundinacea also provide several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
